If participants frequently adjust their strategies or engage in arbitrage, this may be considered more business-related income, and the tax treatment will differ accordingly. 3. Airdropped Tokens: Airdropped tokens issued by DAO projects to contributors are typically considered "incidental income" or "other income." The market liquidity and value of these tokens are highly volatile, and tax authorities typically calculate taxable income based on the market value of the tokens at the time of receipt. III. How to Cope: Tax Preparation for Web3 Income For Web3 practitioners, proactively addressing tax issues and making advance tax plans are key to avoiding future risks. First, ensure the completeness of income declarations, especially in cryptocurrency and Web3 projects where the nature and value of income are highly volatile, requiring timely recording of each transaction. Second, understand how to calculate and declare different types of income, particularly how to handle the accounting of token lock-up periods, exchange rates, and losses. Finally, maintain communication with tax experts to ensure professional answers to tax questions and avoid unnecessary tax risks due to incomplete information or misunderstandings of tax policies. Conclusion As the global tax regulatory environment changes, the tax responsibilities of Web3 practitioners and investors are becoming increasingly clear. While the tax treatment of crypto assets and Web3 income may differ across countries and regions, the trend towards greater global tax transparency makes tracking this income much easier. Therefore, Web3 practitioners should proactively prepare, understand and respond to tax changes in a timely manner, and actively seek professional tax services to ensure compliance and avoid future tax risks.

data.title: Academy of Engineering and Technology of the Developing World (AETDEW) and AB Charity Foundation x AB DAO signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) at the AETDEW Headquarters in Kuala Lumpur on 22 November 2025
data.description:
The Memorandum of Understanding between the Academy of Engineering And Technology of the Developing World (AETDEW) and AB Charity Foundation x AB DAO was signed at the AETDEW Headquarters in Kuala Lumpur on 22 November 2025. The MoU was signed by AETDEW President Ir. Ts. Choo Kok Beng and a representative of AB Charity Foundation x AB DAO, and witnessed by AETDEW Executive Director Dr. Ir. Ts. Wong Chee Fui and Dr. Moneef R. Zou’bi, Senior Advisor to AB Charity Foundation.

Guests attending the signing ceremony also included AETDEW Honorary Fellow and Past President Academician Dato Ir. Lee Yee Cheong, AETDEW Treasurer Mr. Lawrence Tan, and AETDEW Executive Secretary Dr. Yap Kian Lian.

The signing of this Memorandum of Understanding marks the beginning of collaboration between AETDEW and AB Charity Foundation x AB DAO in advancing engineering and technological development, smart grid technologies, and renewable energy systems, with the aim of jointly addressing the energy challenges faced by developing countries.

About AB Charity Foundation

The AB Charity Foundation is an independent, Ireland-registered international non-governmental organization with legal status in the European Union. It is supported by AB DAO through both technological infrastructure and funding. The Foundation is committed to leveraging cutting-edge technologies—such as blockchain and artificial intelligence—to build a transparent, trustworthy, and traceable global infrastructure for philanthropy, promoting sustainable progress in education, healthcare, environmental protection, and humanitarian aid.

AB Charity Foundation website:

https://www.abfdn.org

https://www.ab.org

AETDEW website:

https://www.aetdew.org

data.title: Can L1 tokens compete with Bitcoin in the cryptocurrency space?
data.description:
Author: AJC, Research Manager at Messari; Source: X, @AvgJoesCrypto; Translated by: Shaw, Jinse Finance

Cryptocurrency Drives Industry Development

Refocusing on cryptocurrencies is crucial because this is what most of the industry's funds ultimately try to invest in. The total market capitalization of the cryptocurrency market is $3.26 trillion. Of this, Bitcoin accounts for $1.80 trillion, or 55%.

... Of the remaining $1.45 trillion, approximately $0.83 trillion is concentrated in other Layer-1 protocol (L1) tokens. In total, approximately $2.63 trillion (about 81% of all cryptocurrency funds) has been allocated to assets that the market already considers money or believes may receive a currency premium. Given this, whether you are a trader, investor, capital allocator, or developer, understanding how the market allocates and withdraws currency premiums is crucial. In the cryptocurrency space, nothing influences valuation more than whether the market is willing to treat an asset as money. Therefore, predicting where future currency premiums will accumulate is arguably one of the most important elements in portfolio construction for this industry. So far, we have primarily focused on Bitcoin, but the other $0.83 trillion worth of assets are also worth exploring; they may or may not be considered money. As mentioned earlier, we expect Bitcoin to continue to gain market share from gold and other non-sovereign wealth reserves in the coming years. But what does this mean for L1 tokens? Is it a case of rising tides lifting all boats, or is Bitcoin partially bridging the gap with gold by drawing on the monetary premium from other L1 tokens?

First, it's helpful to understand the current valuation of L1 tokens. The top four L1 tokens are Ethereum ($361.15 billion), XRP ($130.11 billion), BNB ($120.64 billion), and Solana ($74.68 billion), with a total market capitalization of $686.58 billion, accounting for 83% of the entire L1 altcoin market.

... After the top four, valuations drop rapidly (TRX's market capitalization is $26.67 billion), but interestingly, the long tail remains substantial. The total market capitalization of L1 tokens outside the top 15 is $18.06 billion, representing 2% of the entire L1 altcoin market capitalization. Importantly, the market capitalization of L1 tokens does not purely reflect an implied currency premium. Its valuation is primarily based on three frameworks: (i) currency premium, (ii) real economic value (REV), and (iii) the need for economic security. Therefore, a project's market capitalization does not solely stem from the market's perception of it as currency.

Currency premium is the primary driver of L1 valuation, not revenue

Despite these competing valuation frameworks, the market increasingly favors evaluating L1 tokens from the perspective of currency premium rather than revenue-driven valuation. Over the past few years, the overall price-to-sales ratio (P/S) of all L1 tokens with a market capitalization exceeding $1 billion has remained relatively stable, typically between 150x and 200x. However, this overall figure is misleading because it includes Tron and Hyperliquid.

Over the past 30 days, TRX and HYPE generated 70% of the category's revenue but only accounted for 4% of its market capitalization. After removing these two outliers, the truth becomes clear: even with declining revenue, L1 valuations have been rising. The adjusted price-to-sales ratio has also been consistently increasing. A revenue-based explanation might suggest that the market is simply pricing in future revenue growth. However, this explanation doesn't hold up to scrutiny. Within the same L1 token mix (still excluding TRX and HYPE), revenue declined in all years except 2024: 2021 – $12.33 billion 2022 – $4.89 billion (down 60% year-over-year) 2023 – $2.72 billion (down 44% year-over-year) 2024 – $3.55 billion (up 31% year-over-year) 2025 – $1.7 billion (down 52% year-over-year) We believe the simplest and most direct explanation is that these valuations are driven by a currency premium, rather than by current or future revenue. L1 tokens continue to underperform Bitcoin. If the valuation of L1 tokens is driven by expectations of a currency premium, the next step is to understand what factors shape these expectations. A simple test is to compare the price performance of L1 tokens with that of Bitcoin. If the currency premium expectation primarily reflects Bitcoin's price volatility, then these assets should perform similarly to Bitcoin's beta coefficient. On the other hand, if the currency premium expectation is driven by factors specific to each L1 asset, then we expect them to have a much weaker correlation with Bitcoin and perform more idiosyncratically. To better represent the performance of L1 tokens, we examined the performance of the top ten L1 tokens by market capitalization (excluding HYPE) relative to Bitcoin since December 1, 2022. These ten assets represent approximately 94% of the total L1 token market capitalization and are therefore representative of the sector as a whole. During this period, eight of these ten assets underperformed Bitcoin in absolute terms, with six lagging behind Bitcoin by 40% or more. Only two assets outperformed Bitcoin: XRP and SOL. XRP's excess return was only 3%, and given that XRP's historical fund flows have primarily come from retail investors, we will not overemphasize this point. The only asset that significantly outperformed Bitcoin was SOL, with returns 87% higher than Bitcoin. A deeper analysis of SOL's outstanding performance reveals that it may actually be underperforming. During the same period that SOL outperformed Bitcoin by 87%, Solana's fundamentals experienced exponential growth. Decentralized Finance (DeFi) Total Value Locked (TVL) increased by 2988%, transaction fees increased by 1983%, and decentralized exchange (DEX) trading volume increased by 3301%. By any measure, Solana's ecosystem has grown 20 to 30 times since the end of 2022. However, as an asset designed to capture this growth, SOL has only outperformed Bitcoin by 87%.

Read it again.

To achieve significant outperformance against Bitcoin, the L1 cryptocurrency ecosystem needs 2000%-3000% growth to generate double-digit excess returns.

In conclusion, we believe that although L1's valuation is still based on expectations of future currency premiums, market confidence in these expectations is quietly waning. Meanwhile, the market has not lost confidence in Bitcoin's currency premium, and Bitcoin's lead over L1 tokens is widening.

While cryptocurrencies technically do not require fees or revenue to support their valuations, these metrics are crucial for L1. Unlike Bitcoin, L1's core value lies in building an ecosystem (including applications, users, throughput, economic activity, etc.) that supports its token.

However, if L1 ecosystem usage declines year-over-year, partly due to reduced fees and revenue, then the L1 token will lose its only competitive advantage relative to Bitcoin. Without genuine economic growth, the market will find it increasingly difficult to believe in the cryptocurrency narrative of these L1 tokens. Looking ahead, we expect this trend to continue unreversed into 2026 and beyond. With a few exceptions, we anticipate that the market share of L1 altcoin assets will continue to be eroded by Bitcoin. Their valuations are primarily driven by expectations of future currency premiums, which will steadily decline as the market increasingly recognizes Bitcoin as the most competitive of all cryptocurrencies. While Bitcoin will face challenges in the coming years, these issues are unclear and influenced by numerous unknowns, insufficient to substantially support the current currency premiums of other L1 assets. For L1 platforms, the burden of proof has shifted. Compared to Bitcoin, their narratives are no longer convincing, and they cannot rely indefinitely on widespread market frenzy to support their valuations. The era when the idea that "we might one day become money" was enough to justify trillion-dollar valuations is over. Investors now have a decade's worth of data showing that L1's currency premium can only be sustained during periods of rapid platform growth. Aside from these rare periods of explosive growth, L1 tokens have consistently lagged behind Bitcoin, and once growth slows, the currency premium disappears.

data.title: Dubai Blockchain Week: CZ and Schiff engage in heated debate: Bitcoin vs. Gold – which is stronger?
data.description:
I won't go into too much detail about CZ, but I'll mainly introduce Schiff's background.

Peter Schiffwas one of the very few people who publicly warned of the impending US housing bubble and subprime crisis in 2006–2007. He repeatedly engaged in heated debates with "bulls" on mainstream media such as CNN and CNBC, and was known as "Dr. Doom". He rose to fame after the subprime mortgage crisis, but to maintain his popularity, he turned his attention to Bitcoin: In 2013, when Bitcoin was only $300, he called it a "Ponzi scheme, a tulip bubble." In 2017, when Bitcoin was $20,000, he said it would "go to zero immediately." In 2021, when Bitcoin was $6.90, he said it was "the biggest bubble in human history." Even after Bitcoin surpassed $100,000 in 2025, he continued to tweet daily criticizing it, calling it "having no intrinsic value" and "just a bigger fool theory." → This earned him the nickname "Peter 'Bitcoin $0' Schiff" in the crypto community. The Dubai debate on December 4, 2025, was the fulfillment of his long-held wish to "find a crypto guru to fight face-to-face." He proactively tagged CZ on X for several months, finally facilitating this "Gold vs. Bitcoin" showdown of the century. To make it easier for everyone to understand, the essence of the debate is summarized below: 1. Opening Statement CZ (Supports Bitcoin, 7-8 minutes): Bitcoin is an upgraded version of gold. It is digital, divisible, and portable—gold weighs tons and cannot be instantly transferred across borders, while Bitcoin can be done with just a mobile phone. Bitcoin doesn't actually exist anywhere…but that doesn't mean it has no value because it's virtual. Google has value, X (Twitter) has value. Many virtual things have value. Argument 1: Medium of Exchange – Bitcoin transaction fees <$1, Lightning Network makes it <$1 second; “Previously, transfers took days, blockchain transactions take minutes, while gold requires physical transport or expensive banks.” Argument 2: Store of Value – Fixed supply of 21 million coins, inflation-resistant; returns over the past 8 years outpaced gold (although gold outperformed in the last 4 years). “If I send 1 BTC, the transaction is recorded on the blockchain…we know exactly how much there is, and where it is.” Argument 3: Future Potential – Bitcoin integrates DeFi and smart contracts; emerging markets like Venezuela use BTC to escape inflation, something gold cannot do. The fact that institutional ETFs have attracted $50 billion proves its legitimacy. Conclusion: “This is not a zero-sum game, but Bitcoin is the currency of the 21st century. Times have changed, and Bitcoin is the superior currency of the digital age.” Peter Schiff (supports tokenized gold, 7-8 minutes): “Bitcoin is a speculative bubble with no intrinsic value – pure code.” Gold has been proven over millennia and has industrial uses (electronics, jewelry). My Tgold (tokenized gold) combines with blockchain, eliminating the waste of mining. "Bitcoin has no intrinsic value; it's just a number with no practical use, not even for jewelry." Argument 1: Store of Value – Gold is a hedge against crises (e.g., a 25% increase in 2008), while BTC fell 70% in 2022; precious metals have outperformed BTC in the past four years. "Bitcoin's value is determined solely by the next buyer, unlike stocks or businesses that generate income and dividends." Argument 2: Medium of Exchange – Tgold transactions are like BTC, but backed by gold, solving the liquidity problem of gold. Argument 3: Risks – BTC relies on electricity/network and is vulnerable to hacking; gold is physical and cannot be counterfeited. Governments will regulate or ban BTC. Conclusion: "Bitcoin is a casino, Tgold is a bridge – stability + innovation. Choosing stability is not gambling." 2. Cross-examination CZ questions Schiff (7-8 minutes): CZ: Gold storage/transportation costs are high, Bitcoin only requires a wallet. Why not acknowledge the utility of BTC in Africa/emerging markets? Schiff: Emerging markets use USD/Gold (e.g., Indian weddings). BTC is too volatile, not a true payment method. CZ: BTC energy? Renewable >50%, gold mining is heavily polluting. Schiff: BTC Mining is a waste; gold has real value and uses. The debate culminated in a gold bar authentication moment (approximately 2 minutes, eliciting laughter from the audience): CZ took out a 1kg gold bar (worth approximately $70,000), a gift from a Kyrgyz nobleman, and handed it to Schiff: “Peter, is this gold bar real? Let’s verify it.” Schiff carefully examined the markings, color, and weight, but admitted: “I don’t know… I’m not familiar with this mint’s markings. It would require lab testing or a fire assay to confirm. The color is slightly different from my 24K gold bracelet, but that’s not enough to determine.” CZ countered: “Gold is difficult to verify, but Bitcoin is incredibly easy—anyone can verify it anytime using the blockchain, without needing to trust a third party.” Schiff responded: "This is the pain point of gold, but Tgold improves verification through blockchain." Schiff then questioned CZ (7-8 minutes): Schiff: "Bitcoin has no government backing; it collapses if regulations change. Gold has central bank reserves." CZ: "Regulation is a double-edged sword, but Bitcoin's decentralization is its advantage. El Salvador already uses it as fiat currency." Schiff: "If Bitcoin drops to zero, what do you lose? Gold can at least be used to make jewelry." CZ: "The value lies in the network effect. We have over 100 million users, far exceeding gold holders." Schiff sarcastically remarked, "CZ, you've become rich selling Bitcoin, but what about ordinary people?" CZ replied with a laugh, "I never sell; I build the ecosystem." "

3. Free Debate

This part is the most intense, with both sides taking turns speaking, without moderator intervention (15 minutes).

CZ: "Gold increases by 1.7% annually, BTC is fixed. In an era of inflation, who wins? BTC is easy to verify—blockchain is transparent, gold needs to be tested."

Schiff: "BTC is 'digital air,' with no production. Silver price breaks $58, gold is next. Tgold redeems physical gold, not air." CZ demonstrates the Binance Visa card: “When a user swipes the card, cryptocurrency is deducted. From the user's perspective, it's like paying with BTC.” Schiff: “Bitcoin payments? It's just settling bets! You sell BTC to get cash to buy things, you're not actually using BTC. Crypto cards? It's just collateral, not direct payment.” CZ refutes the “Ponzi scheme” argument: “Gold also relies on confidence, what about panic selling? BTC has a HODL culture and global adoption.” Schiff: “BTC mining is wasteful, and regulatory storms are coming. Young people will eventually turn to gold because their friends lost a lot of money on BTC.” CZ: "Young people love BTC because their friends are making money on it, not losing money." 4. Closing Remarks Schiff: "The debate proves: Gold stands the test of time, BTC is short-lived. Choose stability, not gambling. Tgold combines advantages—buy gold now to preserve value! Please list my tokenized gold on Binance." "Young people will love gold even more because their friends are losing a lot on BTC." CZ (5 minutes): "Peter is a legend, but times have changed. BTC is imperfect, but its innovation is like that of the early internet. In the future, BTC will be the global reserve." "BTC is portable, scarce, and programmable. 300 million people are already using it. Thanks to Peter, education wins."
